questions.original_question: 智能驾驶业务（也就是路特斯机器人）的销售额？
questions.answer:
关于路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）自动驾驶业务（即Lotus Robotics）在2024年的具体销售营收，目前没有公开的详细数据。不过，根据2024年11月21日发布的财务报告，路特斯科技在2024年前九个月的智能驾驶业务收入（来自非路特斯品牌客户）达到了1100万美元，同比增长了450%。这表明路特斯在自动驾驶领域的技术应用和商业化取得了显著进展。更多具体数据可能需要等待后续的财务报告披露。

以上信息基于2025年4月22日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: What is the sales revenue of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving business (also known as Lotus Robotics) in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the sales revenue of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving business (also known as Lotus Robotics) in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus achieved a 105% year-on-year increase in topline growth, attributed to growing deliveries. Sales of goods reached $624 million, reflecting a 104% year-on-year increase. Service revenues also surged by 129% year-on-year, totaling $29 million and making up 4.4% of total revenue, an increase from 3.9% in the same period of 2023. Proactive inventory management strategies resulted in a temporary impact on gross margin, but this was partially offset by the growth of the high-margin intelligent driving business. The gross margin of sales of goods declined to 7% for the first nine months of 2024, compared to 10% in the same period of 2023, mainly due to destocking. However, the gross margin of service revenues saw an increase to 55% from 28% during the same period, driven by high-margin technology-related income. The company focused on optimizing operations and organizational structures to improve efficiency while maintaining customer experience.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the sales revenue of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving business (also known as Lotus Robotics) in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Over 12,000 vehicles delivered in 2024, a year-on-year increase of over 70%. Total revenue reached $924 million, a 36% year-on-year growth. Achieved a reduction in operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through rigorous execution of efficiency enhancement initiatives. Lotus Technology is expected to consolidate the entire global business of the Lotus brand by acquisition of a majority interest in Lotus UK upon the closing of Gee. New York – April 22, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2024.

rag_info.sub_query: What is the sales revenue of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving business (also known as Lotus Robotics) in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Announces Preliminary Delivery Results of 2024

metadata.title_summary:
title: Exhibit No.   Description 
summary: Lotus Technology has revealed promising preliminary delivery results for 2024, showcasing a notable rise in vehicle deliveries driven by robust demand for its electric vehicles. This increase is linked to improved production capabilities and effective marketing strategies. The company is focused on maintaining this growth trajectory and enhancing its market presence in the near future. Additionally, they provided insights into specific models and future projections, underscoring their dedication to innovation and customer satisfaction.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. Operating Results 
summary: We are a leading global provider of luxury mobility solutions, focusing on the design and development of sophisticated lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." Our product lineup includes a comprehensive range of Lotus-branded cars, reflecting our commitment to quality in the luxury automotive sector. In 2024, we achieved significant milestones, delivering 12,134 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports models, through our distribution partner LTIL. Our workforce expanded to 1,996 full-time employees, with approximately 52% dedicated to Research and Development across teams in China and Europe. Financially, we experienced substantial revenue growth, with total revenues increasing from US$9.6 million in 2022 to US$924.3 million in 2024. However, this growth was accompanied by net losses, which escalated from US$724.6 million in 2022 to US$1,107.3 million in 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

Lotus Technology is expected to consolidate the entire global business of the Lotus brand by acquisition of a majority interest in Lotus UK upon the closing of Gee. New York – April 22, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter
and Full Year 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, achieved significant milestones by delivering over 12,000 vehicles, which represents a remarkable year-on-year increase of more than 70%. The company's total revenue reached $924 million, reflecting a 36% growth from the previous year. Furthermore, it successfully reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through various efficiency enhancement initiatives. Looking to the future, Lotus plans to strengthen its global presence by acquiring a majority interest in Lotus UK following the completion of the Gee transaction. The unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the full year ending December 31, 2024, were announced on April 22, 2025, in New York.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Third
Quarter and First Nine Months 2024 Financial Results 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. During this period, the company achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 7,600 vehicles, which represents a substantial year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue for the company reached $653 million, more than doubling compared to the same period last year. 

A significant highlight of the financial results was the performance of the intelligent driving business, which generated $11 million in revenue from customers other than Lotus, showcasing an impressive year-on-year growth of 450%. 

In addition to these financial achievements, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its Hyper Hybrid EV technology on November 21, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Robotics reported a revenue of $19 million, which represents a year-over-year growth of 217%. The revenue from other customers soared to $11 million, marking a 450% increase as the external customer base expanded. Additionally, the revenue from Lotus itself grew to $8 million, reflecting a 100% year-over-year growth. The recognized revenue mix for the first nine months of 2024 comprised 58% from other customers and 42% from Lotus. The contract value distribution by region for the same period revealed that 70% originated from China, while Europe and the rest of the world (ROW) accounted for the remaining 30%.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The Group’s revenues are disaggregated by service lines as follows:

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
[Table Level]
- Table Title: Revenue Breakdown by Service Line for Lotus Technology Inc.
- Table Summary: The table presents the disaggregated revenue of Lotus Technology Inc. from different service lines for the years ended December 31 of 2024, 2023, and 2022. It differentiates revenues from sales of goods to third parties and related parties, as well as services provided to third parties and related parties.
- Context: Before the table, it is clarified that the potential common shares associated with certain convertible notes were excluded from the computation due to antidilutive effects. After the table, additional context notes that 'Others' revenue categories include various service-related items.
- Special Notes: Values are presented in thousands of US dollars. Footnotes indicate specific items included in the 'Others' categories for services to third parties and related parties.

[Row Level]
Row 1: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. generated $627,683,000 from selling battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models to third parties, which significantly increased from $464,172,000 in 2023. No revenue was generated from this category in 2022.
Row 2: Sales of sports cars to third parties reached $211,771,000 in 2024, up from $183,160,000 in 2023 and $828,000 in 2022.
Row 3: The sale of parts and other items to third parties resulted in $33,445,000 in 2024, an increase from $9,394,000 in 2023 and $335,000 in 2022.
Row 4: Total sales of goods to third parties amounted to $872,899,000 in 2024, showing an increase from $656,726,000 in 2023 and $1,163,000 in 2022.
Row 5: Revenue from selling BEV lifestyle models to related parties was $6,650,000 in 2024, increasing from $1,093,000 in 2023, with no sales recorded in 2022.
Row 6: Sales of sports cars to related parties accrued $3,318,000 in 2024, up from $951,000 in 2023, and none in 2022.
Row 7: Parts and other items sold to related parties generated $8,194,000 in 2024, a rise from $1,388,000 in 2023, compared to $23,000 in 2022.
Row 8: Sales of goods to related parties totaled $18,162,000 in 2024, increasing from $3,432,000 in 2023 and $23,000 in 2022.
Row 9: A subtotal of $891,061,000 was achieved from total sales of goods in 2024, compared to $660,158,000 in 2023 and $1,186,000 in 2022.
Row 10: Revenue from research and development (R&D) services to third parties was $1,224,000 in 2024, down from $3,448,000 in 2023; there was no income from this category in 2022.
Row 11: 'Others' services to third parties generated $7,706,000 in 2024, up from $2,829,000 in 2023 and $27,000 in 2022.
Row 12: Services to third parties totaled $8,930,000 in 2024, a decrease from $6,277,000 in 2023 and $27,000 in 2022.
Row 13: Revenue from R&D services to related parties was $22,799,000 in 2024, increasing from $10,619,000 in 2023 and $7,800,000 in 2022.
Row 14: 'Others' services to related parties generated $1,559,000 in 2024, down from $1,954,000 in 2023 and $544,000 in 2022.
Row 15: Services to related parties produced a subtotal of $24,358,000 in 2024, compared to $12,573,000 in 2023 and $8,344,000 in 2022.
Row 16: Total service revenue across third and related parties reached $33,288,000 in 2024, up from $18,850,000 in 2023 and $8,371,000 in 2022.
Row 17: The total revenues for Lotus Technology Inc. summed to $924,349,000 in 2024, showing an increase from $679,008,000 in 2023 and $9,557,000 in 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by the company.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In recognition of Lotus Technology Inc.’s leadership in intelligent driving, the Company recently received approval to test Level 3 autonomous driving in Wuxi, a city near Shanghai serving as China’s pilot zone for “internet of vehicles” connectivity. This license allows Eletre drivers to use the Eletre’s Level 3 functionality on designated highways in the city. The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. As announced in January 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has entered into a definitive agreement and plan of merger (the “Merger Agreement”) with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement are expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2024, and would result in Lotus Technology Inc. becoming a public company. Upon completion of the business combination, the combined company is expected to retain Lotus Technology Inc.’s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: $4 ^ { \intercal \mathsf { H } }$ QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2024 KEY NUMBERS 
summary: In 2024, Lotus achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 12,000 vehicles, which is an impressive year-on-year increase of more than 70%. This surge in deliveries contributed to a total revenue of $924 million for the year, reflecting a robust 36% growth compared to the previous year's revenue of $679 million. A closer look at the revenue figures reveals that in the fourth quarter of 2023, Lotus reported $361 million, a significant decline of 25% compared to the $272 million earned in the same quarter of 2024. Despite economic challenges in China, Lotus's strong market performance, particularly in European and North American markets, underscores the brand's resilience and growth trajectory in the automotive industry.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 23. REVENUES 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Revenues for Lotus Technology Inc.**

For the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenues of $398,115, marking a substantial increase from $130,035 during the same period in 2023. This growth is attributed to significant advancements in both product sales and service offerings, categorized into sales to third parties and related parties.

**Sales to Third Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Revenue surged to $262,849 in 2024, up from $72,790 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Sales rose to $102,618 in 2024, compared to $49,146 in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $15,096 in 2024 from $2,111 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Third Parties:** Totaled $380,563 in 2024, a significant rise from $124,047 in 2023.

**Sales to Related Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Generated $535 in 2024, slightly up from $436 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Recorded sales of $91 in 2024, with no sales in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $1,704 in 2024 from $371 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Related Parties:** Reached $2,330 in 2024, compared to $807 in 2023.

**Combined Sales Subtotal:** The total revenue from goods sold to both third and related parties was $382,893 in 2024, a notable increase from $124,854 in 2023.

**Services Provided:**
- **R&D Services to Third Parties:** Generated $56 in 2024, with no revenue in 2023.
- **Other Services to Third Parties:** Valued at $4,504 in 2024, compared to $160 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Third Parties:** Totaled $4,560 in 2024, up from $160 in 2023.
- **R&D Services to Related Parties:** Increased to $6,366 in 2024 from $4,983 in 2023.
- **Other Services to Related Parties:** Rose to $4,296 in 2024 from $38 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Related Parties:** Reached $10,662 in 2024, compared to $5,021 in 2023.

**Total Services Subtotal:** The combined revenue from services provided to both third and related parties was $15,222 in 2024, up from $5,181 in 2023.

This comprehensive revenue analysis underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust growth across various product and service lines, reflecting its expanding market presence and improved operational performance. All figures are presented in thousands of US dollars, with footnotes clarifying additional service categories.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Robotics’ mission is to accelerate the transition to self-driving technology by creating endless opportunities for how people and goods move using intelligent vehicles and robotics. Lotus Robotics offers end-to-end autonomous driving services including the software for self-driving, autonomous testing capabilities, and fleet management offerings on the cloud.

metadata.title_summary:
title: About Lotus Robotics 
summary: Lotus Robotics is focused on accelerating the transition to self-driving technology by offering advanced solutions for transporting people and goods via intelligent vehicles and robotics. Their services encompass a wide range of autonomous driving capabilities, including self-driving software, autonomous testing, and cloud-based fleet management.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: It was also anticipated that in 2025, China, the UK, the EU, and the US would contribute approximately 43%, 16%, 20%, and 12% of the total sales volume respectively (with the rest of the world, including Asia (other than China), Australia, certain parts of the Middle East, and South America, contributing approximately 9% of the total sales volume). • Expected average selling prices, which were anticipated to vary across markets. The series of assumptions included, among other things, the selling prices of each vehicle model reflecting Lotus Technology’s pricing strategy as well as its analysis of regional consumer preferences, competitive landscape, the sales and distribution arrangements in respective markets, the configurations of different models as well as the price of future models. In addition to vehicle sales revenue, Lotus Technology also expected to generate revenue from vehicle aftersales services and new energy vehicle (NEV) credit sales, which were directly linked to the projected vehicle sales revenue. Furthermore, Lotus Technology also expected to generate revenue from the sale of used vehicles. • Technology-related revenue captured the revenue generated from the sale of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving software, and fees earned from licensing its Electric Performance Architecture (EPA) platform and other advanced technologies. Revenue generated from the sale of Lotus Technology's autonomous driving software was primarily driven by the anticipated cumulative delivery of its lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) and external subscription base as well as the expected take rate in respective regions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Guided by Lotus Technology's R&D leaders, the global research and development team has extensive experience in the automotive and technology industries. As of June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology's R&D team consisted of 1,631 professionals with extensive knowledge in automotive engineering, software, and artificial intelligence, as well as diversified working experiences from leading vehicle manufacturers globally. Such composition ensures solid technology development capabilities, especially in intelligence and digitalization. The diversified cultural and professional background promotes the exchange of ideas from different perspectives and ensures the generation of innovations. Lotus Technology established Lotus Robotics in China, the arm of its in-house intelligent driving competence. Lotus Robotics is committed to developing advanced intelligent driving technologies to foster global industry growth. Lotus Robotics has established itself as a global premier provider of comprehensive intelligent driving solutions, including advanced intelligent driving platform software, cloud toolchains, integrated hardware and software development, and intelligent driving operation solutions. Starting from 2024, Lotus Robotics has been providing end-to-end intelligent driving solutions to several leading automakers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Worldwide Research and Development Footprint**

Lotus has established a robust global Research and Development (R&D) team dedicated to driving innovation across key technological domains, with a particular emphasis on electric vehicle (EV) technologies. As of June 30, 2024, this team consists of 1,631 professionals who possess expertise in automotive engineering, software development, and artificial intelligence (AI). Their collective skills ensure that Lotus maintains strong capabilities in technology development, especially in areas related to intelligence and digitalization.

The company is actively advancing its 800-volt Electric Power Architecture (EPA) and associated technologies to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles in the luxury automotive market. In addition to improving its own product offerings, Lotus aims to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual property and software solutions to other luxury automotive brands, thereby expanding its revenue streams.

In a strategic move to bolster its technological prowess, Lotus has established Lotus Robotics in China, which functions as the company's in-house division for intelligent driving technologies. Since its inception in 2024, Lotus Robotics has been delivering comprehensive intelligent driving solutions to major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. This positions Lotus as a leading provider in the global market for intelligent driving solutions, further solidifying its commitment to innovation and technological advancement in the automotive sector.

Overall, Lotus's R&D initiatives reflect a strategic alignment with the evolving demands of the automotive industry, particularly in the realms of electric mobility and autonomous driving technologies, supported by a well-structured and highly skilled team.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus achieved a 105% year-on-year increase in topline growth, attributed to growing deliveries. Sales of goods reached $624 million, reflecting a 104% year-on-year increase. Service revenues also surged by 129% year-on-year, totaling $29 million and making up 4.4% of total revenue, an increase from 3.9% in the same period of 2023. Proactive inventory management strategies resulted in a temporary impact on gross margin, but this was partially offset by the growth of the high-margin intelligent driving business. The gross margin of sales of goods declined to 7% for the first nine months of 2024, compared to 10% in the same period of 2023, mainly due to destocking. However, the gross margin of service revenues saw an increase to 55% from 28% during the same period, driven by high-margin technology-related income. The company focused on optimizing operations and organizational structures to improve efficiency while maintaining customer experience.

Lotus Robotics has established itself as a global premier provider of comprehensive intelligent driving solutions, including advanced intelligent driving platform software, cloud toolchains, integrated hardware and software development, and intelligent driving operation solutions. Starting from 2024, Lotus Robotics has been providing end-to-end intelligent driving solutions to several leading automakers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Worldwide Research and Development Footprint 
summary: The organization features a dedicated global team that enhances its research and development (R&D) efforts across various technological domains, with a strong emphasis on collaboration between experienced teams in Europe and China. This innovation strategy is geared towards advancing critical electric vehicle (EV) technologies, supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem. Key initiatives include the development of cutting-edge technologies like the 800-volt EPA architecture, as well as hardware, algorithms, and software systems aimed at boosting vehicle competitiveness. The organization also plans to monetize its intellectual property and software through licensing and subscription models targeted at luxury automotive brands. As of December 31, 2024, the global R&D team, led by seasoned professionals, consists of 1,030 experts in automotive engineering, software, and AI, whose diverse backgrounds contribute to enhanced technology development, particularly in intelligence and digitalization. Additionally, Lotus Robotics, established in China, is at the forefront of intelligent driving technology, providing a comprehensive suite of solutions including advanced software, cloud toolchains, and integrated development services. Since 2024, Lotus Robotics has been delivering complete intelligent driving solutions to major automakers like Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto, further solidifying its role in the global automotive industry.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: From 2023 to 2025, gross margin is expected to increase from approximately 14% – 16% in 2023 to approximately 21% – 23% in 2025, reflecting Lotus Technology’s increase in operational efficiency and economies of scale as well as higher revenue contribution from technology-related revenue with higher projected gross profit margin. • Projected EBITDA was based on a variety of operational assumptions further to the assumptions applied to revenue and gross profit estimation, including, among others, assumptions regarding research and development expenses, general and administrative expenses, selling and marketing expenses, and others. The research and development expense forecast reflected the expenses incurred for electrical architecture development, new model development and design, forward-looking technology projects, intelligent cockpit projects, and autonomous driving projects. The selling and marketing expense forecast reflected Lotus Technology’s advertising and marketing strategy, Lotus Technology’s expansion plan of sales and distribution network in the global markets, commissions to Lotus partners, etc. Lotus Technology was expected to achieve a positive EBITDA margin of over 5% in 2025, driven by the increase in gross profit margin and the decrease of operating expenses as a percentage of revenue.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: From 2023 to 2025, gross margin is expected to increase from approximately 14% – 16% in 2023 to approximately 21% – 23% in 2025, reflecting Lotus Technology’s increase in operational efficiency and economies of scale as well as higher revenue contribution from technology-related revenue with higher projected gross profit margin. • Projected EBITDA was based on a variety of operational assumptions further to the assumptions applied to revenue and gross profit estimation, including, among others, assumptions regarding research and development expenses, general and administrative expenses, selling and marketing expenses, and others. The research and development expense forecast reflected the expenses incurred for electrical architecture development, new model development and design, forward-looking technology projects, intelligent cockpit projects, and autonomous driving projects. The selling and marketing expense forecast reflected Lotus Technology's advertising and marketing strategy, Lotus Technology's expansion plan of sales and distribution network in the global markets, commissions to Lotus partners, etc. Lotus Technology was expected to achieve a positive EBITDA margin of over 5% in 2025, driven by the increase in gross profit margin and the decrease of operating expenses as a percentage of revenue.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
[Table Level]  
- Table Title: Reconciliation of Net Loss to Adjusted Net Loss and Adjusted EBITDA for the Years Ended December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024  
- Table Summary: The table provides a breakdown of sales revenue and service revenue across different categories for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024. It also highlights the changes in revenue between these years, expressed both in absolute terms and as percentages.  
- Context: The table presented comes after an explanation of Lotus Technology's approach to financial measurement and performance reconciliation. Changes in revenue figures, including sales of goods and services, are influenced by increased sales in BEV lifestyle models, sports cars, parts, and intelligent driving service income.  
- Special Notes: All figures in the table are in thousands of US dollars, except percentage changes.  

[Row Level]  
Row 1: For the year ended December 31, 2024, the sales of goods amounted to US$891,061 thousand, an increase of US$230,903 thousand, or 35.0%, from US$660,158 thousand in the previous year.  
Row 2: BEV lifestyle models generated US$634,333 thousand in revenue in 2024, representing a growth of US$169,068 thousand from US$465,265 thousand in 2023, marking a 36.3% increase.  
Row 3: Revenue from sports cars reached US$215,089 thousand in 2024, up by US$30,978 thousand from US$184,111 thousand in 2023, reflecting a 16.8% rise.  
Row 4: Other sales contributed US$41,639 thousand to the revenue in 2024, showing a significant increase of US$30,857 thousand compared to US$10,782 thousand in 2023, translating to a growth rate of 286.2%.  
Row 5: Service revenues totaled US$33,288 thousand in 2024, a rise of US$14,438 thousand, or 76.6%, from US$18,850 thousand in 2023.  
Row 6: Total revenues summed up to US$924,349 thousand for the year ended December 31, 2024, increasing by US$245,341 thousand, or 36.1%, from US$679,008 thousand in the previous year.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Non-GAAP Financial Measures 
summary: The company employs adjusted net loss and adjusted EBITDA as essential metrics to evaluate its operational performance and inform financial and operational decisions. Adjusted net loss is derived by excluding share-based compensation from net loss, while adjusted EBITDA further refines this by excluding interest income, interest expenses, income tax expenses, depreciation, and share-based compensation. These non-GAAP measures are presented to assist management in assessing business performance and formulating strategies, as they can highlight underlying trends that may be masked by certain expenses.

In 2024, the company reported a net loss of US$1,107,289 thousand, an increase from US$750,255 thousand in 2023 and US$724,563 thousand in 2022. The adjusted net loss for 2024 was US$1,075,359 thousand, up from US$750,255 thousand in 2023. Key adjustments included share-based compensation expenses of US$31,930 thousand in 2024, interest expenses rising to US$58,218 thousand, and depreciation costs increasing to US$76,488 thousand. Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was US$960,930 thousand, reflecting fluctuations over the years.

The company also reported significant revenue growth, with total revenues reaching US$924,349 thousand in 2024, a 36.1% increase from US$679,008 thousand in 2023. This growth was driven by increased sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, sports cars, and intelligent driving services. Sales of goods rose to US$891,061 thousand, primarily due to a substantial increase in BEV lifestyle model sales and a notable rise in service revenues, which grew by 76.6% to US$33,288 thousand.

Overall, while adjusted metrics provide valuable insights into the company's financial performance, they should be considered alongside GAAP measures for a comprehensive understanding. The reconciliation of net loss to adjusted figures highlights the adjustments made, underscoring the importance of a thorough review of financial information rather than relying solely on individual metrics.
